title: Computer Vision AI Engineer
description:
Company Intro:

Our world is immersive and visceral. Content today is static
and boring. We are Momenti, video that you interact with,
bringing visceral experiences to all content. Build deeper
connections and emotions with multi-dimensional video, and
bring life to content by breaking the 4th wall. Join the
content revolution! The world is alive, and so should your
captured moments. Momenti is here.

Job Summary:

We are looking for an experienced, motivated Computer Vision
AI Engineer to join our NY team! In this role, you will be
researching and applying a range of software, computer
vision and machine learning techniques for solving
challenges that bridge virtual and real worlds.

Key Responsibilities:

Develop, test, debug, deploy, and maintain computer vision
algorithms and hardware for different environments. Develop
automated vision algorithms, especially for work with robots
and autonomous hardware systems. Gather and optimize
analytics from computer vision algorithms to improve their
performance

Preferred Qualifications:

• computer vision frameworks (OpenCV, MediaPipe, etc)

• building and operating MLOps

• experience in any generative computer vision AI such as
stable diffusion model

• experience in 3D recon AI

Basic Qualification:

• degree in computer science, electrical engineering or
applied mathematics

• 3+ years of hands on experience in classification of
sequence of images ( action recognition) with ViT or RCNN or
any sequential image classification deep learning model
(must have)

• fluent coding in Python

• deep learning frameworks (Tensorflow, Keras, Pytorch, etc)

Note: primary candidates only, no agencies, placement firms,
or recruiters please
responsibilities: Develop, test, debug, deploy, and maintain computer vision algorithms and hardware for different environments
Develop automated vision algorithms for robots and autonomous hardware systems
Gather and optimize analytics from computer vision algorithms to improve performance
Research and apply software, computer vision, and machine learning techniques for challenges bridging virtual and real worlds
use_cases: Action recognition on sequences of images for multi-dimensional video content
Autonomous robot navigation and hardware interaction
Interactive video experiences that break the fourth wall
ai_classification_reasoning:
The role focuses on traditional computer vision and deep
learning techniques like ViT, RCNN, and action recognition
on image sequences. While it mentions generative AI as a
preference, the core requirements are conventional computer
vision algorithms and models for robotic/autonomous systems,
not LLMs, RAG, or agentic AI.
